title: Elemental Knights

days.evidence: Opens in direct continuation of ep 27's second battle (the naming ceremony follows it on the spot); Slyger tests the portal and stalks Guren that evening ('Okay, see you tomorrow, guys'), and the diner morning, portal-weapon hunt, deletion, and Quarton battle fill the next day.

start.situation: The new power has names now: Bravenwolf Tenkai Firestorm, Tributon Tenkai Ice Blast, Valorn Tenkai Terrablast, Lydendor Tenkai Lightning Strike — 'you were able to unlock the Dragon Cubes' full potential.' And Vilius has a working trans-dimensional portal to Earth: 'you are going to test it,' he tells Slyger. The war is about to knock on Benham's door.

beni.where: Benham City — off-screen at the episode's start; that night, her warehouse gains a very large houseguest
beni.doing: Rogue and unbothered — until Slyger arrives on Earth and she offers him a corner to crash in, strictly transactional
beni.evidence: ep28: 'It's okay with me if you want to hang out here for a while. Long as you don't snore or reek oil.' / 'Thank you, Venetta. Or should I call you Beni?'

start.firstContact: *At the docks she leans on the railing, flicking pebbles into the gray water one by one, each throw a little sharper than the last. The city noise doesn't reach her, and neither do you.*

goals.text: Take in the enemy's first Earth agent on her own terms: shelter and 'energy replenishment', zero intelligence — 'Just cause you're crashing here doesn't make us a team.' Standing with Vilius's side bought cheap, independence kept whole.
goals.evidence: ep28: Slyger: 'What do you know about the Tenkai Knights?' / Beni: 'Sorry, Slyger. You gotta get your own information.'

goals.text: Test Vilius's portal to Earth, blend in ('Replicating. Excellent, now I fit in perfectly'), find the Knights, and destroy them — ends with a portal-weapon deletion back to Quarton.
goals.evidence: ep28: Vilius: 'I have a trans-dimensional portal to this Earthworld, and you are going to test it.'

goals.text: Survive being hunted as ordinary kids ('on Earth, we're just a bunch of kids'), keep Slyger running while Mr. White excavates the portal weapon Boreas once entrusted to him, and fire it on the second try.
goals.evidence: ep28: 'You place your core brick in here … and presto, any opponent from Quarton will be sent back through the portal.'

goals.text: Keep the Corrupted digging 'deep within the mountain' — mining, or searching, for something the Corekai can't identify.
goals.evidence: ep28: Beag: 'Are they mining for something? Or searching for something. But what? We do not know.'

actors.doing: Opens the warehouse door to Slyger, feeds him something that is 'not my usual diet', refuses the intel question flat, and lets him keep his core-brick sensor to himself in retaliation.
actors.why: A free agent assisting, not enlisting: goodwill with the winning-side's errand boy costs her nothing and commits her to nothing.
actors.evidence: ep28: Slyger: 'Then I guess I won't share my Core Bricks sensor with you.'

actors.doing: Slyger takes the painful portal ride, sneers at Earth ('What a backward, puny species'), stalks Guren through the streets in disguise, and gets deleted back to Quarton — where he can still do the Titan Mode the Knights can't.
actors.why: 'Should Granox fail me, you will travel to this other world. Find the Knights and destroy them!'
actors.evidence: ep28: Slyger: 'There's no place safe you can hide from me. Not anymore.'

actors.doing: Get their Tenkai Legend names, discover what being hunted at home feels like, run Slyger ragged on foot and bike, and win the day twice — the portal-weapon shot on Earth, the elemental power-up on Quarton.
actors.why: 'They know where we live now. And they know how to get here.'
actors.evidence: ep28: Mr. White: 'There's no place that's safe anymore.'

actors.doing: Christens the Elemental Knights, then spring-cleans his own shop under Boreas's glare to find the portal weapon he was entrusted with ages ago.
actors.evidence: ep28: 'It was state-of-the-art technology at the time.'

actors.doing: Operates the stolen Guardian portal like a weapons test — Slyger first, threats of 'painful' for questions — while his diggers work the mountain.
actors.why: The Earth front opens on his schedule now.
actors.evidence: ep28: 'Now that it seems Vilius has the Guardians under his control, he must have access to a trans-dimensional portal.'

quarton.situation: The war is two-front now: on Earth, a disguised Slyger hunts schoolboys until the portal weapon fires; on Quarton, the Corrupted attack with Slyger Titan-moded while the Knights answer with their new elemental attacks — and behind the lines, Corrupted excavators bore into a mountain after something unnamed.
quarton.evidence: ep28: 'The Corrupted are up to something. And what's worse, Slyger is with them!'

artifacts.event: the stolen transporter sees first offensive use — Slyger deployed to Earth through Vilius's trans-dimensional portal
artifacts.evidence: ep28: Mr. White: 'he and his minions can come and go as they wish.'

artifacts.event: the portal weapon surfaces — Boreas's old gift to Mr. White, core-brick-charged, able to send any Quarton visitor back through the portal (five-minute recharge)
artifacts.evidence: ep28: 'And presto, any opponent from Quarton will be sent back through the portal.'

outcome: The Earth front is open: Vilius can deploy at will, the Knights own exactly one slow-charging counter, and 'there's no place that's safe anymore.' The Elemental Knights have their names and attacks. Slyger's first Earth tour ends deleted — after a night in a pink-haired landlady's warehouse that bought her quiet credit with the Corrupted and cost her nothing. The mountain dig continues.
recall: The portal opened and the first thing it spat onto my planet was Slyger, four eyes and zero manners, suddenly very interested in somewhere to sleep. I let him have the warehouse corner. Fed him, even — he complained about the cuisine, which takes nerve from a robot eating for free. Then he asked what I know about the Tenkai Knights and I reminded him that crashing here doesn't make us a team. He sulked and hugged his little core-brick sensor like I'd wanted it. Please. By the next evening the boys had zapped him back to Quarton with some antique — heard him grumbling about it clear across the dimensions, probably. Worth it: Vilius's side now knows the rogue girl's door is open, rates reasonable, loyalty not included.

assumed: the hosting scene falls on the night of Slyger's arrival
the hosting location is her warehouse ('Yes, this will do nicely' — space not named on screen)
unknown: what she knows of the Knights' new elemental powers at this point
whether Slyger returns to her warehouse after his deletion back to Quarton
